An anonymous trader bought YES for 22 countries in the 2026 World Cup at 1.7¢ each, investing $20K in each position. 

The total investment is approximately $440K.

Profile:[https://t.co/RcTjivezfL]

The logic is simple: if at least one country wins the tournament, the entire bet pays off with interest.

France is already trading at 23¢ - the position is up 854%, with a current profit of $166K from that trade alone.

Spain +533%, England +515%, Brazil +221%.

This is a classic strategy for prediction markets - a uniform distribution at the start of the tournament, when the market has not yet factored in the actual odds.

Loss-making positions -Belgium, Morocco, the U.S., Japan, and Switzerland - have already been effectively written off, but their combined loss is less than a single profitable move by France.

Currently on @futuredotnews France remains the favorite, but thanks to Messi’s spectacular play in this tournament, the Argentine national team is hot on its heels

THIS GUY TURNED 8 INTO 5,372 ON JUST ONE POLYMARKET WEATHER MARKET

Profile: [https://t.co/tX6b97IFY1]

HOW THE STRATEGY WORKS:

Polymarket opens hundreds of weather markets every day - "Will the temperature be exactly X°C in City Y?"

The price of most of them drops to 0.1–2¢ because the average participant doesn't know the exact forecast.

buracosolar systematically monitors weather forecasts for São Paulo, Busan, Seoul, and Mexico City - cities with predictable climates and a large number of open-air markets.

When the forecast aligns with an undervalued market price, he enters the trade.

WHY IT WORKS:

→ Open weather data - ECMWF, AccuWeather, local stations

→ The market cannot keep up with pricing within narrow temperature ranges (+-1°C)

→ At a price of 0.1¢, even $8 yields 6,500 contracts -and a 650x return if you win

→ Spreads the risk: most bets are between $5 and $200, rarely more

1,749 predictions, $11,771 in profit. No big capital - just a clear understanding of what the market is mispricing.

This isn't trading. It's automated market inefficiency arbitrage. 

Polymarket makes this possible - for now.

This unknown guy enters the weather markets with $20–200 and walks away with $500–2,000.

He trades exclusively in the temperature markets on Polymarket - London, Paris, and New York.

Not crypto. Not the election. The weather.

Why? Because most people ignore these markets - liquidity is low, and prices are inefficient.

He takes advantage of that.

The strategy is simple:

1/ Identifies markets where prices deviate significantly from the actual weather forecast

2/ Bets a small amount - $20–200 - when the odds seem too low

3/ Awaiting confirmation from several weather services before entering

4/ Never bet more than necessary - the position size is minimal, but the odds are huge

It buys items for 2–57 cents each that cost $1.

His top 3 deals:

> $76 - $2 069 (+2 614%)
> $571 - $2 336 (+309%)
> $129 - $1 734 (+1 242%)

He doesn't predict the weather - he identifies where the market is wrong.

The market says, "There's a 3% chance of 31°C in London."

Weather data suggests: significantly more.

He sets the price. The market pays.

A foolproof two-layer strategy on Polymarket's weather markets that earned this guy $59,097 in profit.

Profile: [ https://t.co/pHRuhTPlK4 ]

Best Deals:

$37,38 → $15 182,37 (+40 511%)
$4,88 → $1 220,81 (+24 900%)
$108,34 → $1 487,17 (+1 272%)

4,900 predictions over 5 months. The largest single bet was $15.1K.

What's the strategy? He plays at both ends at the same time.

The first layer consists of lottery tickets. He buys them for 0.2-7 cents in situations where the market has almost no confidence in the outcome. Most of them lose.

But one winning bet - and he's up $15K on his $37 investment.

The second tier consists of markets that are nearly saturated.

He buys shares for 30-99 cents where the outcome is already clear.

A lower profit margin, but stable and predictable.

Result: A steady stream of high-probability bets protects against losses, while rare big wins from low-stakes bets account for all the actual profit.

Hong Kong single-handedly made up for dozens of failures with room to spare.

Not luck. Portfolio mathematics.
